questions.question: In the Full Range Leadership Development (FRLD) model, which leadership style is considered the LEAST effective?
options: Management by Exception (Active)
Contingent Reward
Management by Exception (Passive)
Laissez-Faire

questions.question: Which FRLD leadership behavior involves a leader who avoids making decisions, abdicates responsibilities, and is absent when needed?
options: Laissez-Faire
Management by Exception (Passive)
Contingent Reward
Individualized Consideration

questions.question: In the FRLD model, Management by Exception (Passive) is characterized by a leader who:
options: Actively monitors performance and takes corrective action before problems become serious
Provides rewards in exchange for satisfactory performance
Challenges followers to think creatively and question assumptions
Intervenes only after problems have become serious or standards have not been met

questions.question: A supervisor who actively monitors subordinate performance and takes corrective action BEFORE problems become serious is demonstrating which FRLD behavior?
options: Management by Exception (Active)
Management by Exception (Passive)
Contingent Reward
Inspirational Motivation

questions.question: Which FRLD transactional leadership behavior involves clarifying expectations and providing rewards when goals are achieved?
options: Individualized Consideration
Contingent Reward
Management by Exception (Active)
Inspirational Motivation

questions.question: Which of the following correctly lists the four transformational leadership behaviors in the FRLD model?
options: Contingent Reward, Individualized Consideration, Intellectual Stimulation, and Inspirational Motivation
Laissez-Faire, Management by Exception, Contingent Reward, and Idealized Influence
Individualized Consideration, Intellectual Stimulation, Inspirational Motivation, and Idealized Influence
Self-Awareness, Self-Regulation, Empathy, and Social Skills

questions.question: A leader who treats each follower as an individual, acts as a mentor, and pays attention to the unique developmental needs of each person is demonstrating which transformational behavior?
options: Intellectual Stimulation
Idealized Influence
Inspirational Motivation
Individualized Consideration

questions.question: Which transformational leadership behavior involves encouraging followers to question assumptions, reframe problems, and approach old situations in new ways?
options: Intellectual Stimulation
Inspirational Motivation
Idealized Influence
Individualized Consideration

questions.question: Inspirational Motivation, as described in the FRLD model, is best characterized by a leader who:
options: Monitors subordinate work for errors and takes corrective action
Avoids involvement and lets things work themselves out
Articulates a compelling vision, communicates high expectations, and uses symbols to focus efforts
Provides individual coaching and developmental opportunities

questions.question: Idealized Influence in the FRLD model refers to a leader who:
options: Serves as a role model, earns trust and respect, and demonstrates high ethical standards
Relies primarily on position power and formal authority
Delegates all decision-making to subordinates
Focuses exclusively on task completion and mission metrics

questions.question: From LEAST effective to MOST effective, which sequence correctly orders the FRLD leadership behaviors?
options: Management by Exception (Passive), Laissez-Faire, Contingent Reward, Management by Exception (Active), Idealized Influence, Inspirational Motivation, Intellectual Stimulation, Individualized Consideration

Laissez-Faire, Management by Exception (Passive), Management by Exception (Active), Contingent Reward, Individualized Consideration, Intellectual Stimulation, Inspirational Motivation, Idealized Influence

Contingent Reward, Management by Exception (Active), Management by Exception (Passive), Laissez-Faire, Individualized Consideration, Intellectual Stimulation, Inspirational Motivation, Idealized Influence

Idealized Influence, Inspirational Motivation, Intellectual Stimulation, Individualized Consideration, Contingent Reward, Management by Exception (Active), Management by Exception (Passive), Laissez-Faire

questions.question: Which of the five core components of Emotional Intelligence involves the ability to recognize and understand your own emotions, strengths, weaknesses, values, and drives?
options: Self-Regulation
Empathy
Social Skills
Self-Awareness

questions.question: A leader who can control or redirect disruptive impulses and moods, and thinks before acting, is demonstrating which component of Emotional Intelligence?
options: Self-Awareness
Motivation
Self-Regulation
Empathy

questions.question: In the context of Emotional Intelligence, which component involves a passion to work for reasons beyond money or status and a propensity to pursue goals with energy and persistence?
options: Self-Awareness
Social Skills
Empathy
Motivation

questions.question: Empathy, as a component of Emotional Intelligence, is best described as:
options: The ability to manage relationships and build networks
The ability to understand the emotional makeup of other people and treat them according to their emotional reactions
The ability to recognize your own emotions in real time
The ability to control impulsive feelings and behaviors

questions.question: Which component of Emotional Intelligence involves proficiency in managing relationships, building networks, finding common ground, and building rapport?
options: Social Skills
Empathy
Motivation
Self-Awareness

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Airman Comprehensive Assessment (ACA)?
options: To replace the Enlisted Performance Report as the official evaluation tool
To serve as a formal disciplinary counseling session
To provide supervisors and subordinates with a structured opportunity to discuss professional development, personal goals, and duty performance
To determine eligibility for promotion testing

questions.question: How often must supervisors conduct an Airman Comprehensive Assessment (ACA) with their subordinates at a minimum?
options: Monthly
Annually
Quarterly
Semi-annually

questions.question: An initial ACA feedback session should be conducted within how many days of assuming supervision of an Airman?
options: 60 days
30 days
90 days
14 days

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes the difference between mentoring and coaching?
options: Mentoring is required by regulation; coaching is always informal
Coaching is a long-term relationship; mentoring is a one-time session
There is no difference; the terms are interchangeable in Air Force doctrine
Mentoring focuses on long-term career development and is typically a voluntary relationship; coaching focuses on improving specific job performance and near-term tasks

questions.question: Counseling, as distinct from coaching and mentoring, is primarily used when:
options: A subordinate needs long-term career guidance and professional development
A subordinate's performance or behavior has fallen below acceptable standards and must be corrected
A subordinate is performing well and needs to be challenged with new responsibilities
A subordinate requests guidance on which PME course to attend

questions.question: According to situational leadership concepts, which factor should MOST influence a leader's chosen style?
options: The leader's personal preference for directive or delegative approaches
The rank of the subordinate
The maturity and competence level of the follower relative to the task
The number of subordinates in the work center

questions.question: A supervisor notices a subordinate consistently arriving late to work. The supervisor documents the behavior and has a private conversation explaining the standard and the impact of the behavior. This is an example of:
options: Mentoring
Delegation
Coaching
Counseling

questions.question: Which type of feedback session is conducted midway through the reporting period to inform the ratee how well they are meeting duty performance expectations?
options: Midterm feedback
Initial feedback
Ratee-requested feedback
Follow-up feedback

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about ratee-requested feedback?
options: It may only be requested during the first 60 days of supervision
Supervisors are not required to honor a ratee's request for feedback
It replaces the requirement for an initial feedback session
The ratee may request feedback at any time, and supervisors should accommodate the request

questions.question: Ethical leadership in the Air Force requires leaders to:
options: Prioritize mission accomplishment over all other considerations, including ethical standards
Adhere to the Air Force core values of Integrity First, Service Before Self, and Excellence in All We Do
Make decisions based primarily on personal convictions rather than regulatory guidance
Enforce standards selectively based on the situation

questions.question: Effective delegation requires a leader to:
options: Assign the task with appropriate authority while retaining overall accountability for the outcome
Assign the task and all associated accountability to the subordinate
Only delegate to subordinates who have previously performed the exact task
Avoid providing guidance so the subordinate can learn independently

questions.question: Which statement about followership is most consistent with Air Force leadership doctrine?
options: Effective followers passively comply with all directives without question
Followership is only relevant for Airmen in the junior enlisted tier
Effective followers are critical thinkers who actively support the organization and are willing to provide candid input to leaders
Followership skills are separate from and unrelated to leadership skills

questions.question: Empowerment in a leadership context means:
options: Allowing subordinates to operate without any oversight or boundaries
Transferring all leadership responsibilities to the most experienced team member
Providing detailed step-by-step instructions for every task
Giving subordinates the authority, resources, and confidence to make decisions and take action within defined boundaries

questions.question: In the FRLD model, transactional leadership behaviors include:
options: Idealized Influence and Inspirational Motivation
Intellectual Stimulation and Individualized Consideration
Contingent Reward and Management by Exception (Active and Passive)
Laissez-Faire and Contingent Reward

questions.question: A SNCO observes that a newly assigned SSgt has strong technical skills but lacks confidence in leading a team. The SNCO begins meeting regularly with the SSgt to share experiences, provide career guidance, and help develop long-term leadership goals. This is an example of:
options: Mentoring
Counseling
Coaching
Delegation

questions.question: A supervisor works with a subordinate to improve a specific skill needed for an upcoming inspection by observing performance, providing real-time feedback, and suggesting techniques. This is an example of:
options: Mentoring
Coaching
Delegation
Counseling

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic that distinguishes transformational leadership from transactional leadership?
options: Transformational leaders inspire followers to exceed expectations by appealing to higher ideals and values
Transformational leaders rely primarily on rewards and punishments to motivate followers
Transactional leaders inspire followers to transcend self-interest for the good of the organization
Transactional leaders avoid setting clear expectations for subordinates

questions.question: According to AFH 1, standards of conduct for Air Force leaders include all of the following EXCEPT:
options: Maintaining professional relationships
Avoiding the appearance of impropriety
Using government resources for personal convenience when it does not interfere with the mission
Ensuring fair and equitable treatment of all personnel

questions.question: Which of the following five elements make up the core components of Emotional Intelligence?
options: Integrity, Service, Excellence, Courage, and Commitment
Self-Awareness, Self-Regulation, Motivation, Empathy, and Social Skills
Planning, Organizing, Leading, Controlling, and Evaluating
Vision, Trust, Communication, Delegation, and Accountability

questions.question: During an initial feedback session, what should a supervisor primarily communicate to the ratee?
options: Performance expectations, duty responsibilities, and how the ratee will be evaluated
The ratee's projected promotion score
A summary of the ratee's previous assignment performance
The specific decoration the ratee can expect at the end of the reporting period

questions.question: A leader who avoids getting involved until problems become chronic and then only reacts to correct failures is demonstrating which FRLD behavior?
options: Laissez-Faire
Management by Exception (Active)
Contingent Reward
Management by Exception (Passive)

questions.question: Which of the following scenarios best illustrates Intellectual Stimulation?
options: A flight chief tells the team exactly how to complete a task using the established checklist
A section chief asks team members to brainstorm innovative solutions to a recurring maintenance problem and challenges them to question existing procedures
A supervisor promises a day off in exchange for meeting the weekly production goal
A superintendent shares a personal vision of what the unit can achieve in the next year

questions.question: In the context of leadership competencies, what does the Air Force expect of NCOs regarding professional development of subordinates?
options: NCOs should focus exclusively on mission tasks and leave development to officers
NCOs should only develop subordinates who specifically request assistance
NCOs are expected to actively develop subordinates through feedback, mentoring, and providing opportunities for growth
Professional development is the sole responsibility of the Education and Training office

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes the relationship between the FRLD model's Laissez-Faire style and Management by Exception (Passive)?
options: Both are equally effective forms of transactional leadership
Management by Exception (Passive) is less effective than Laissez-Faire
They are identical behaviors with different names

Laissez-Faire represents complete avoidance of leadership, while Management by Exception (Passive) involves reacting to problems only after they occur — both are ineffective but Laissez-Faire is the least effective
